Linker script template for defining memory layout and sections for Pebble applications.

# Purpose
The content is a linker script used in the build process of a software application. It defines the memory layout and section allocation for the application binary. The `ENTRY(main)` directive specifies the entry point of the application. The `MEMORY` block declares a memory region named `APP` with read, write, and execute permissions, and its size is determined by the `@MAX_APP_MEMORY_SIZE@` placeholder. The `SECTIONS` block organizes the binary into different sections such as `.header`, `.note.gnu.build-id`, `.text`, `.data`, and `.bss`, each mapped to the `APP` memory region. The script also includes a `DISCARD` section to exclude specific libraries and sections like `libc.a`, `libm.a`, `libgcc.a`, and `.eh_frame` from the final binary.
